channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Landed for 1.8.7 and 2.2

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,client,el5,inkernel #359
LU-624 Kernel update [RHEL6.1 2.6.32-131.17.1.el6] (Revision 8c5062286668cb6da9db7293e2c5329ad3f7803b)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : 8c5062286668cb6da9db7293e2c5329ad3f7803b
Files :

  • lustre/ChangeLog
  • lustre/kernel_patches/which_patch
  • build/lbuild-rhel5
  • lustre/kernel_patches/targets/2.6-rhel6.target.in
  • build/lbuild-rhel

Brian, Could you please tell us why we disable signmodule for rhel6?

Well, it's not specific to rhel6 but is inherited by rhel6 from the generic rhel spec patching code and has lineage back to the initial building of the rhel5 kernel from the vendor's SRPM, all done in commit 0fdc61b8eb9c42939b086f221d4bdb9d0a6c9caf.

Since I have a policy of only making the most minimal modifications to the upstream vendor's code when doing these sorts of things I can only assume that at the time, kernel module signing was not possible or was causing problems.
Update patch to enable signmodule for rhel6: http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,1632
Hi, Mike, you are right. It really not relate to build environment. Brian, Could you please tell us why we disable signmodule for rhel6?

This is a problem in the build chain, not the build environment.

In older EL6 kernels, there is a default crypto/signature/key.h shipped in the vanilla tarball. In the EL6.1 kernels, this file has been stripped out.

In kernel.spec, we have this block:

%if %{signmodules}
...
gcc -o scripts/bin2c scripts/bin2c.c
scripts/bin2c ksign_def_public_key __initdata <extract.pub >crypto/signature/key.h
%endif

However, in lbuild, we have this:

   sed -i -e 's/^\(%define signmodules \).*/\10/' \

So we're explicitly disabling module signing, therefore the header is never generated, and builds fail. I'm not sure what the correct solution is, but this shows that it's not a problem with the build node OS.
Move up to trace 2.6.32-131.17.1.el6 kernel.

Integrated in lustre-b1_8 » x86_64,server,el5,inkernel #132
LU-624 Kernel update [RHEL6.1 2.6.32-131.12.1.el6] (client only)

Johann Lombardi : 6b320ae244221aac2f63a2c3f94842c1db5b3099
Files :

  • lustre/kernel_patches/targets/2.6-rhel6.target.in
  • lustre/ChangeLog

Hi, Mike, Looks like we need update osimage to rhel6.1 or just update gcc? Thanks for your help.

+ /usr/lib/rpm/debugedit -b /var/lib/jenkins/workspace/lustre-reviews/arch/x86_64/build_type/server/distro/el6/ib_stack/inkernel/BUILD/BUILD -d /usr/src/debug -i arch/x86/vdso/vdso32-sysenter.so.dbg
4d5fe926a0b2ca4d66776d59669c54adac6af1fb
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c:2:17: error: key.h: No such file or directory
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c: In function 'ksign_init':
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c:10: error: 'ksign_def_public_key' undeclared (first use in this function)
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c:10: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c:10: error: for each function it appears in.)
crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.c:11: error: 'ksign_def_public_key_size' undeclared (first use in this function)
make[2]: *** [crypto/signature/ksign-publickey.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [crypto/signature] Error 2
make: *** [crypto] Error 2
make: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
error: Bad exit status from /var/tmp/rpm-tmp.1dsaVT (%build)

http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/2065/arch=x86_64,build_type=server,distro=el6,ib_stack=inkernel/console
